title: Aureline docs citation-anchor object model
description: Boundary schema for the docs_citation_anchor_record frozen in docs/docs_integrity/citation_and_reference_contract.md. Every docs-search hit, guided-tour step, glossary card, onboarding step, docs-pane footer, AI-explanation overlay citation, hosted-review evidence row, repair-packet citation, and portability / offline export citation that needs to reconstruct 'what was cited, from which source class, against which pack revision, under which locale, at which freshness and version-match posture, with which browser-handoff reason if any' resolves to exactly one docs_citation_anchor_record. The record is a strict superset of the minimal citation_anchor_record reserved in schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json (ADR 0013) for badge-inline use: help_status_badge_record.citation_anchor_refs point into anchor ids that resolve against this richer shape. Raw page bodies, raw symbol definitions, raw runbook bodies, and raw URLs never cross this boundary; this schema carries opaque ids, typed vocabulary, and locale / freshness / version-match / browser-handoff-reason metadata only. The eventual docs-integrity crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new anchor kind, target-ref kind, reuse-surface class, derivation class, or export-posture value is additive-minor and requires a docs_citation_contract_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

docs_citation_contract_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the docs citation and symbol-linked-reference family. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version. The same version integer gates schemas/docs/symbol_linked_reference.schema.json so citation-anchor and symbol-linked-reference records evolve lock-step.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries this kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests / portability exports. Raw URLs, raw page bodies, raw runbook bodies, and raw symbol definitions never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

bcp47_locale_tag.description: BCP-47 language tag (e.g. en, en-US, pt-BR, zh-Hant-HK). Re-exported from schemas/docs/docs_pack_manifest.schema.json without modification.

source_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json without modification (ADR-0013). Citation-anchor records whose source_class = derived_explanation MUST declare derivation = derived or derived_with_upstream_anchors and MUST point upstream_citation_anchor_refs at least one anchor whose source_class is one of project_docs, generated_reference, mirrored_official_docs, curated_knowledge_pack, support_runbook, or release_note (via release_note_anchor). external_status_feed and vendor_provider_docs anchors alone are insufficient backing for a derived explanation.

anchor_kind.description: Frozen citation-anchor-kind vocabulary. Superset of the ADR-0013 anchor_kind vocabulary with the glossary_term_anchor and onboarding_step_anchor additions reserved for the docs-integrity lane so glossary cards and guided tours carry one typed anchor class instead of minting parallel reference shapes. A derived-explanation record MUST cite at least one upstream anchor in the authoritative subset (docs_page_anchor, generated_reference_anchor, symbol_reference_anchor, runbook_step_anchor, release_note_anchor, mirrored_docs_anchor, curated_pack_anchor).

freshness_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011 / ADR-0013). Captured at mint time so exports and support handoffs can reconstruct the posture the user saw, even when live refresh is unavailable.

version_match_state.description: Re-exported from schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json without modification (ADR-0013). Captured at mint time against the running build identity (D-0011) so a later reviewer can recompute whether the cited revision was authoritative when the citation was produced.

redaction_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011 / ADR-0007). The broker-owned redaction pass runs before bytes reach any persistent or exportable sink.

browser_handoff_reason.description: Re-exported from schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json without modification (ADR-0010 subset). Required (non-null) when the anchor's target resolves via an out-of-product browser handoff (vendor_provider_anchor, external_status_feed_anchor, or a mirrored_docs_anchor whose canonical owner is a web portal). Null otherwise. Raw URLs MUST NOT live on the anchor; the rendering surface quotes the frozen schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json envelope by packet id.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Inline reference to the ADR-0010 browser_handoff_packet that the anchor exposes for an out-of-product action. Required (non-null) when browser_handoff_reason is non-null. Null otherwise.

policy_context.description: Policy context the anchor was minted under (ADR-0001 trust state, ADR-0008 policy epoch, ADR-0009 execution-context id).

derivation.description: Frozen derivation taxonomy. primary means the anchor is the authoritative reference (the row is the canonical source). derived means the anchor is synthesized from other anchors (e.g. an AI-explanation paragraph) and MUST carry at least one upstream_citation_anchor_refs entry with an authoritative source_class. derived_with_upstream_anchors is a composite used when a derived explanation spans multiple authoritative anchors (multi-pack quotes, cross-locale paraphrase). A derived / derived_with_upstream_anchors anchor with an empty upstream_citation_anchor_refs array is non-conforming and MUST be denied by the publisher with the ADR-0013 derived_explanation_uncited degraded-state cause.

reuse_surface.description: Frozen reuse-surface vocabulary naming the protected surfaces this anchor may be reused on. Superset of schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json surface_class with glossary_card, hosted_review_evidence, repair_packet_evidence, portability_export, and offboarding_export added so the docs-integrity lane can enumerate which surfaces copied / quoted an anchor without inventing parallel vocabularies. Surfaces not named in reuse_surfaces MUST NOT project the anchor into a user-visible row; the anchor is carried through as an opaque id for audit and reconstruction only.

export_posture.description: Frozen export posture naming what a portability / offboarding / support-bundle export does with the anchor. retain_full carries every field through. retain_opaque_only carries only anchor_id, pack_revision_ref, and target_build_identity_ref so a later reviewer can reconstruct the row without locale or wording leakage. strip_for_redaction_class removes the anchor entirely from the export and renders a typed 'anchor redacted under <redaction_class>' disclosure in its place. A redaction_class of signing_evidence_only or internal_support_restricted MUST raise the posture to strip_for_redaction_class on metadata_safe_default exports; surfaces MAY widen the posture only when the operator / admin has opted into a broader-capture export.

target_kind.description: Discriminator naming which typed ref the anchor resolves. MUST agree with the sibling *_ref field carried on this record.

symbol_ref.description: Opaque pin to the symbol id (fully-qualified symbol identity in the project's symbol graph). Raw source-file bytes are never carried here.

code_span_ref.description: Opaque pin to a code span (file / symbol + span id). The anchor carries only the opaque span id; raw buffer bytes are fetched from the canonical owner on demand.

target_ref.description: Typed target reference. Exactly one typed *_ref field is populated; the anchor_kind MUST agree with the target_kind (e.g. anchor_kind=symbol_reference_anchor pairs with target_kind=symbol). Raw paths, raw URLs, and raw source bytes never appear here.

pack_id.description: Opaque stable id of the docs pack this anchor resolves into, matching docs_pack_manifest_record.pack_id. Lets a reviewer recover the canonical owner even when pack_revision_ref has been withdrawn or superseded.

pack_revision_ref.description: Opaque pin to the docs-pack revision the anchor was minted against (matches docs_pack_manifest_record.pack_revision_ref).

locale.description: Locale the cited content was rendered in at mint time. Exports and support handoffs preserve this so a reviewer knows which locale row to reconstruct; a later parity audit can compare the anchor's locale against the pack's locale_coverage row.

target_build_identity_ref.description: Opaque pin to the running build identity (D-0011) the anchor was minted under. Paired with pack_revision_ref and target_ref this is sufficient to reconstruct 'what row the user saw' offline.

client_scopes.description: Client classes the anchor was minted for. Re-exported from ADR-0011. An anchor whose client_scopes exclude the rendering surface MUST NOT render as authoritative there; the surface denies with the ADR-0013 client_scope_excludes_surface degraded-state cause.

upstream_citation_anchor_refs.description: Upstream anchor ids this citation derives from. MUST be empty for derivation = primary. MUST be non-empty for derivation = derived or derived_with_upstream_anchors; at least one upstream anchor MUST resolve to an authoritative source_class (project_docs, generated_reference, mirrored_official_docs, curated_knowledge_pack, support_runbook) or a release_note_anchor; citing only vendor_provider_anchor or external_status_feed_anchor is non-conforming and the publisher MUST deny with derived_explanation_uncited.

reuse_surfaces.description: Surfaces the anchor is authorised to project into. A rendering surface that is not named here MUST NOT render the anchor as a user-visible row; it may still carry anchor_id through as opaque audit metadata.

browser_handoff_reason.description: Required (non-null) when the anchor's canonical owner resolves via an out-of-product browser handoff. Null otherwise.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Required (non-null) when browser_handoff_reason is non-null. Null otherwise.

superseded_by_anchor_id.description: Opaque pin to a later anchor that supersedes this one (typically after a pack revision bump or a rename-refactor). When set, surfaces that reuse this anchor MUST render the typed 'superseded' disclosure and offer the migrate_to_replacement repair hook. Reviewers can still reconstruct the superseded row for historical audit.

allOf.description: derivation = derived and derived_with_upstream_anchors MUST name at least one upstream_citation_anchor_refs entry; primary MUST have none.
browser_handoff_packet_ref MUST be non-null when browser_handoff_reason is non-null.
source_class = derived_explanation MUST declare derivation = derived or derived_with_upstream_anchors.

anchor_kind and target_ref.target_kind MUST agree: a symbol_reference_anchor MUST resolve to a symbol target; a docs_page_anchor to a docs_page target; a code_span_anchor to a code_span target; a runbook_step_anchor to a runbook_step target; a release_note_anchor to a release_note target; a service_health_event_anchor to a service_health_event target; a glossary_term_anchor to a glossary_term target; an onboarding_step_anchor to an onboarding_step target. mirrored_docs_anchor / curated_pack_anchor / vendor_provider_anchor / external_status_feed_anchor / generated_reference_anchor accept docs_page, symbol, code_span, or release_note targets.

docs_citation_anchor_record.description: Docs citation anchor projecting one citation across docs panes, the docs browser, Help / About, service-health rows, support summaries, onboarding steps, AI-explanation overlays, glossary cards, hosted-review evidence, repair packets, and portability / offboarding exports. Reviewers reconstruct what was cited by pairing pack_id + pack_revision_ref + target_ref + target_build_identity_ref with the anchor's locale and freshness_class_at_mint. A derived explanation MUST retain upstream anchors; flattening an AI or glossary paragraph to prose-only authority is non-conforming.
